witnesses.description: One exact bisection step for f(x) = x^2 - 2 on [1, 2].
One exact Newton step for f(x) = x^2 - 2 from x = 3/2.

checks.claim: The listed interval update is one exact bisection step for x^2 - 2.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the midpoint, polynomial values, sign-changing subinterval, and width.

checks.claim: The listed Newton iterate from x = 3/2 is 17/12.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes f(x), f'(x), and x - f(x)/f'(x) exactly.
The validator recomputes both residuals over exact rationals and checks the strict decrease.

checks.claim: The Newton step from x = 3/2 for x^2 - 2 is 4/3.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes the next iterate as 17/12; the source artifact checks that the same iterate cannot also be 4/3.

checks.claim: Bisection and Newton convergence theorems require future Lean or numerical-honesty resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: existence and uniqueness of roots, bisection convergence, Newton convergence rates, and floating-point stability guarantees
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus separate numerical-honesty metadata for floating-point runs

checks.notes: The finite rows check only listed rational iterates; general convergence and floating-point claims remain outside this pack.
